findings.alpha_provenance: pre-registered in the playbook [stats] table; approved with the plan at Human Gate 1, before any p-value was computed
findings.bh_note: significance flags use Benjamini-Hochberg-adjusted p-values across the full family of tests this stage ran; raw p-values are reported for re-performance, never for the flag. A reported 0.0 means the value is below the 6-decimal rounding contract's resolution (p < 1e-06), not literally zero.

independence.assumption: every hypothesis test above treats rows as independent observations
independence.scan_rule: profiled columns outside the analysis with >= 20 distinct values and >= 5.0 average rows per value

methods.contingency_rxc: Pearson chi-square, correction=False (scipy.stats.chi2_contingency); validity per Cochran's rule (NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook)
methods.effect_sizes: Cramer's V (contingency), rank-biserial r (Mann-Whitney; Kerby 2014) - always reported alongside p (ASA Statement on p-values, 2016, principle 5)
methods.multiple_comparisons: Benjamini-Hochberg FDR across all hypothesis tests in this stage (Benjamini & Hochberg 1995; statsmodels multipletests fdr_bh)
methods.numeric_two_group: Mann-Whitney U, two-sided, method='auto' (scipy.stats.mannwhitneyu); parametric t-test refused in v1 - normality is an assumption this engine cannot certify
methods.proportion_interval: Wilson score (Brown, Cai & DasGupta 2001; NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook 7.2.4.1; statsmodels proportion_confint)

findings.positive_class_rule: lexicographically last of the two observed levels - a disclosed deterministic rule, not a guess
findings.proportions_note: intervals are INDIVIDUAL (per-estimate) Wilson intervals, not simultaneous confidence bands - declared, not hidden
findings.target_selection: first binary_target in plan column order (plan approved at Human Gate 1); candidates listed under target_candidates

mde.note: smallest effect this test could reliably detect at the pre-registered alpha and the declared power; a non-significant result rules out effects above this size, not the existence of an effect
